items.title: Nigel Wong re-elected Matta president for second term
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nigel Wong Chun Teim has been re-elected as president of the Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents (Matta) for the 2026-2029 term, marking his second term leading the association. The new Executive Council (exco) members elected at the association's 51st Annual General Meeting held at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur on Saturday, comprises seven leaders from the previous term and three new members. Meanwhile, Wong said he was honoured to be re-elected as Matta president and thanked members for their confidence in the new leadership team.

items.title: Venezuela search teams scramble as hope fades of finding quake survivors
items.summary: LA GUAIRA, Venezuela: Hopes were fading Monday of finding survivors more than four days after powerful twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as residents grow increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the disaster that has killed at least 1,450 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for. Some 774 buildings were badly damaged in back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 that struck on Wednesday evening, including 189 buildings that have totally collapsed, National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez said on Sunday. Last week, UN aid chief Tom Fletcher said that more than 50,000 people were missing.

items.title: Trump: Iran requested meeting to be held in Doha on Tuesday
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said that Iran has requested a meeting that will be held in the Gulf state of Qatar on Tuesday, despite an earlier denial from Teheran that talks were planned. "IRAN HAS REQUESTED A MEETING. Trump posted on his Truth Social platform on Monday.

items.title: Rehda Malaysia elects Zaini Yusoff as new president for 2026-2028 term
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Real Estate and Housing Developers' Association (REHDA) Malaysia has elected Datuk Zaini Yusoff as its new president for the 2026-2028 term, succeeding Datuk Ir Ho Hon Sang following the association's annual delegates conference on June 27, 2026. REHDA said that Ho, who had served as REHDA Malaysia president since June 2024, actively engaged stakeholders from the public and private sectors to address issues affecting the real estate industry during his tenure. Besides Chong's appointment as deputy president, the new leadership line-up also comprises Datuk Charlie Chia Lui Meng and Datuk Ir Tiah Oon Ling as vice-presidents.

items.title: Couple arrested in RM3.59mil Johor drug bust
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have dismantled a drug syndicate in Kulai after arresting a married couple and seizing narcotics worth an estimated RM3.59 million. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the seized drugs could have fed the habits of more than 90,000 users. His wife tested negative for drugs.

items.title: GTA inks partnerships to strengthen aerospace talent pipeline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global Turbine Asia Sdn Bhd (GTA) has formed partnerships with Universiti Teknikal Malaysia Melaka (UTeM), AMRACE Sdn Bhd and PT3 Consultant Sdn Bhd to bolster talent development and industry collaboration in aerospace sector. "It is about building a stronger aerospace support ecosystem that can produce future-ready talent, support career transitions and enhance industry capabilities. "GTA believes sustainable industry growth must be anchored in skilled talent, hands-on training and close collaboration between industry, academia and training partners," she said.

items.title: Teen assaulted in Tumpat remains traumatised, MP clarifies not rape case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A teenage girl who was robbed and assaulted in an incident in Palekbang, Kelantan, recently remains traumatised, Tumpat MP Datuk Mumtaz Md Nawi told the Dewan Rakyat. The victim, she said, managed to escape during an attempted rape but suffered serious injuries, including facial fractures after being beaten with a stick. "The teenager was attacked in an attempted rape but managed to escape.

items.title: Johor election: State Bersatu chief foresees post-election coalition talks
items.summary: PAGOH: Johor Bersatu chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal believes no single coalition is likely to secure an outright majority in the upcoming Johor election, raising the prospect of post-election cooperation to form the next state government. The former menteri besar said Perikatan Nasional (PN) remained confident of performing well despite contesting only 33 of the 56 state seats. The former Johor PN chief said leaders and members within the coalition remained focused on strengthening cooperation among its component parties.

items.title: Consulate: Malaysians told to be on alert amid heavy rain warning in southern Thailand
items.summary: SONGKHLA, June 29 — The Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla is urging Malaysians in southern Thailand and those travelling to the province to be on alert, following a heavy to very heavy rain forecast in several areas of the country from June 28 to July 3. Malaysian citizens requiring consular assistance may contact the duty officer at the Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla by calling +66 81-990-1930 or e-mailing mwsongkhla@kln.gov.my. The Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla also informed that it would continue to monitor the weather and situational developments in Thailand and convey the latest information as required.

items.title: Ajinomoto Malaysia names advisers for privatisation bid by Japanese parent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd has appointed Maybank Investment Bank Bhd as principal adviser for its proposed privatisation by parent company Ajinomoto Co Inc. RHB Investment Bank Bhd will act as independent adviser for minority shareholders, Ajinomoto Malaysia said in a Bursa Malaysia filing on Monday. The appointments follow last week's proposal by Ajinomoto, which owns a 50.38 per cent stake or 30.63 million shares in Ajinomoto Malaysia, to take the company private via a selective capital reduction (SCR) and repayment exercise at RM20 per share. Under the proposal, Ajinomoto will pay RM603.4 million to buy and cancel 30.17 million shares held by minority shareholders through the SCR.

items.title: Sim racing gains ground as industry expands
items.summary: SIM racing is steadily emerging as one of Malaysia's fastest-growing esports segments, supported by increasing government recognition and growing corporate interest. H1GP Sim Racing Championship founder Mika Hakimi said the esports has evolved significantly from being viewed merely as a video game to becoming a legitimate competitive platform with pathways into professional motorsport. "Sim racing definitely peaked during the Covid-19 period, and growth has remained healthy since then.

items.title: Johor election: PH, PN candidates cross paths at night market during campaign
items.summary: PAGOH: Patrons at the Taman Sri Pagoh night market were treated to an unusual sight on the third day of campaigning for the Johor election when two rival candidates crossed paths while greeting voters. Perikatan Nasional candidate for Bukit Kepong, Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal, and Pakatan Harapan candidate for Bukit Pasir, Muhd Najib Lep, briefly found themselves campaigning in the same area, drawing curious looks from traders and visitors at the bustling night market. "The Bukit Pasir candidate is actually an old friend.

items.title: Malaysian Media Council seeks PSC review of Cybercrimes Bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has called for the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 to be referred to a parliamentary select committee for further scrutiny and improvement, as it infringes on privacy, including using covert operations to obtain private communications. "The Cybercrimes Bill 2026 lacks the safeguards enclosed in international conventions that the bill is aimed to meet. The bill grants extensive powers relating to computer system access, the preservation of computer data, the interception of communications, real-time traffic data collection, search and seizure, and covert investigative activities.

items.title: RHB Bank fully redeems US$500 mil senior unsecured notes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Bank Bhd has fully redeemed its existing US$500 million senior unsecured notes issued under its Euro Medium Term Note (EMTN) Programme. In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the bank said the senior unsecured notes, which were issued on June 29, 2021, have been fully redeemed at their nominal value. Following the redemption, the notes will be delisted from the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Ltd and Labuan International Financial Exchange Inc. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Advance Profinity unveils oil change scheme with insurance, warranty coverage
items.summary: ADVANCE Profinity Sdn Bhd, a distributor of lubricant products, has introduced Automaster Care which combines engine oil change services with insurance and warranty coverage. Under the programme, customers who use the company's engine oil at participating workshops will receive four months of personal accident protection. The company is also offering an engine oil warranty programme that provides up to RM10,000 in engine repair coverage.

items.title: PM: 25,000 Malaysians will benefit from new Bakat Madani initiative
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 29 — A total of 25,000 people are set to benefit from the Bakat Madani initiative launched today, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the initiative is a national effort involving government-linked investment companies (GLICs), government-linked companies (GLCs), and Petronas to boost national talent development, focusing on skills development, the creation of quality jobs and improving social mobility. Meanwhile, Amir Hamzah said the initiative emphasises three main areas: strengthening employability and career placement within the GLIC, GLC and Petronas ecosystem; expanding job placement opportunities across key strategic sectors; and empowering Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions.

items.title: PM: Malaysia, Indonesia committed to strengthening cooperation in legislation, governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia and Indonesia are committed to strengthening cooperation in the fields of legislation, governance and various strategic areas of mutual interest, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he discussed the matter with Indonesia’s Coordinating Minister for Legal, Human Rights, Immigration and Correction, Prof Dr Yusril Ihza Mahendra, who paid him a courtesy call at the Prime Minister’s Office in Putrajaya today. During the meeting, Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said both leaders also exchanged views on efforts to strengthen cooperation in those fields.

items.title: Pas tells Johor voters: Don't vote for PH, defend Malay-Muslim power
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas has told voters not to pick Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the upcoming Johor election in its bid to defend Malay-Muslim political power in the state. He added that voters should not cast their ballots for PH, especially in constituencies where PH contests against Barisan Nasional (BN) in straight fights. Earlier today, Pas Youth said that the party will back BN candidates in seats where Perikatan Nasional (PN) is not contesting, in a move to block PH.

items.title: Johor election: Bersatu only issues directive to vote for PN candidates
items.summary: MUAR: Bersatu has not issued any directive allowing its members and supporters to vote for candidates in seats not contested by Perikatan Nasional in the Johor election. If PN is contesting, we support PN. Also present was PN candidate from Bersatu for Bukit Kepong, Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal.

items.title: Australian pipeline assessment technology lands in Malaysia via Insights Analytics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Insights Analytics Bhd has secured the exclusive rights to distribute and deploy an Australian-developed pipeline condition assessment technology across Malaysia. This gave the company exclusive rights to commercialise, deploy and provide after-sales support for PIA's proprietary pipeline condition assessment technology suite called, p-CAT. Insights Analytics managing director Frank Wee said the partnership marks a significant step in the company's mission to bring world-class pipeline diagnostic technology to Malaysia.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 29, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Monday, although the FBM KLCI staged a notable recovery from its intraday lows after heavy selling pressure in the morning session. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded lower at RM244,228. Following the trend, Ethereum traded lower at RM6,417, while Solana gained to RM296.

items.title: Court orders mental assessment of man who hurt grandmother with hammer
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Sessions Court today ordered a 25-year-old man charged with causing grievous hurt to his grandmother with a hammer to undergo a mental health assessment. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli issued the order against Muhammad Nor Iman Abdullah following an application by the prosecution, which requested that the accused undergo a psychiatric evaluation before any plea or statement is recorded. The court ordered him to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak, to undergo a mental assessment.

items.title: SC issues new guidance note to strengthen Maqasid alignment in sukuk market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commissson (SC) has issued the guidance note on sukuk to reinforce how its issuances align with Maqasid al-Syariah principles. While current frameworks already ensure Shariah compliance, the new guidance note introduces a more holistic approach to evaluating sukuk. It encourages Syariah advisers to look beyond compliance and consider broader value creation including alignment with issuers' strategic goals as well as wider economic and social outcomes.

items.title: Johor election: BN must build own strength, not rely on others
items.summary: Its deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said every political party must develop its own strength, as only strong parties can earn public confidence and attract support from the people. Mohamad, fondly known as Tok Mat, however, said the coalition would not reject any party that sincerely wishes to help by voting for BN candidates. "If there are parties that genuinely want to help and sincerely support BN candidates, we thank them.

items.title: Couple hospitalised after wild mushroom meal in Padang Besar
items.summary: SIK, June 29 — A husband and wife from Padang Besar were rushed to hospital after suffering severe food‑poisoning symptoms believed to be caused by eating wild mushrooms picked near a cattle shed at their home. Berita Harian reported that the couple, Muhd Basir Abidudin Abd Razak and Intan Nabila Mazelam, both 28, reportedly cooked the mushrooms for lunch before collapsing hours later. The mushrooms were later determined to be poisonous.

items.title: Johor polls: EC issues over 24,000 postal ballot papers
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Election Commission (EC) has issued 24,677 postal ballot papers for the 16th Johor election. EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the issuance of postal ballots was conducted today by election officials across all 56 state constituencies. He said the 24,677 postal ballots comprised 23,288 for Form 1A applicants, which include election officials, EC members and officers; police and armed forces personnel; and media practitioners.

items.title: Bakat Madani set to benefit 25,000 in first year, says Anwar
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Putrajaya's newly-launched Bakat Madani initiative is expected to benefit 25,000 individuals within a year, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. The initiative focuses on enhancing employability and career placement opportunities within government-linked investment companies (GLICs), government-linked companies (GLCs) and Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas). It also seeks to expand job opportunities across the country's strategic sectors.

items.title: Europe's deadly heatwave scorches eastern flank, takes aim at Ukraine
items.summary: PARIS: The most severe heatwave ever recorded in Europe roasted central and eastern parts of the continent on Monday and began to creep over Ukraine, which scrambled to prepare its war-ravaged power grid for the shock of scorching heat. France reported at least 74 drowning deaths since June 18 and Poland said 17 drowned on Sunday alone. The state weather service said the country would face "intense heat", with temperatures of 35C-38C expected on Monday.

items.title: Pas Youth: Vote shift to BN in some seats aimed at blocking PH
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pas Youth has clarified that the party's readiness to back Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in seats where Perikatan Nasional (PN) is not contesting is a strategic move to block Pakatan Harapan (PH). Pas Youth information chief Nadzir Helmi said the statement by the Pas deputy president must be understood within a broader political strategy. "Pas Youth believes that breaking PH's dominance is a necessity that all parties must understand.

items.title: Parliament sends condolences to senator Awang Bemee over mother's death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul today conveyed Parliament's condolences to Dewan Negara president Senator Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah following the death of his mother. "It is with deep sadness that I inform the House that the mother of Dewan Negara President Senator Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah, Sehat Rasdi, died a short while ago," he said. "I hope Senator Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah and the entire family remain strong during this difficult time.

items.title: After viral Sabah hospital post, Sabah Health Dept cites familiar strain and overcrowding
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 29 — Sabah's Health Department has responded to a viral Facebook post depicting the crowded conditions at Queen Elizabeth Hospital (QEH), highlighting the overstretched public hospital’s infrastructure and manpower. The statement came after a Facebook user named Sualim Gopog shared his experience accompanying a family member to the hospital's Emergency and Trauma Department (ETD) in the early hours of the morning including slow moving queues and many people found sleeping in the lobby and outside the hospital building. In the widely shared post , Gopog described seeing dozens of people sleeping on benches and on the floor of the corridors, relatives searching for food after the hospital canteen closed at 12am, near-impossible parking, and patients waiting hours to be seen.

items.title: Plus Xnergy launches one of Malaysia's largest self-consumption BESS
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Plus Xnergy Services Sdn Bhd (Plus Xnergy) has commissioned one of Malaysia's largest single-deployment self-consumption (battery energy storage systems (BESS) to optimise electricity consumption. Plus Xnergy group chief executive officer Ko Chuan Zhen said the project delivery marks a significant milestone in Malaysia's clean energy transition. Developed in collaboration with global energy storage solutions provider HyperStrong, Plus Xnergy said the 20 megawatt-hour BESS has reached full commercial operation at a major food manufacturing facility in Perak.

items.title: Gaza health officials say 3 killed in Israeli drone strike
items.summary: GAZA CITY: Gaza health officials said an Israeli drone strike killed three civilians and injured several others on Monday, the latest violence to hit the Palestinian territory despite a ceasefire. "Three people were killed and several injured when an Israeli drone struck a group of civilians," Al-Aqsa hospital said in a statement. Gaza's civil defence agency, which acts as a rescue force under Hamas, said the strike hit an area in Deir el-Balah in central Gaza.

items.title: Strengthening resilience and energy security in Malaysia
items.summary: AS United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres underscored during the recent London Climate Action Week, the climate and energy crises must be addressed together and at their root. In Malaysia, that transition is already taking shape through a growing set of policies and initiatives that link climate ambition with energy security, economic competitiveness and resilience. The National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) therefore provides strategic direction for Malaysia's broader economic and energy transformation, linking decarbonisation with competitiveness, investment and long-term energy security.

items.title: Bomb blast in Narathiwat: Wisma Putra confirms no Malaysian casualties
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 29 — Malaysia’s Foreign Ministry is monitoring developments in southern Thailand after an improvised explosive device (IED) detonated in Narathiwat province today, with a Malaysian‑registered vehicle reportedly caught in the blast. The explosion occurred at about 11.45am local time, according to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA). In a preliminary update, Wisma Putra said no Malaysian fatalities have been reported, and the Consulate General in Songkhla is in contact with Thai authorities to verify details and extend consular assistance where needed.

items.title: Johor polls: BN manifesto realistic, youths the biggest winners, says leader
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Barisan Nasional (BN) manifesto for the 16th Johor election is a realistic blueprint that champions youth by focusing on their actual needs, says Johor BN Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros. He said the manifesto, launched by Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, avoids empty promises and offers actionable commitments. Noor Azleen, who is also Johor Umno Youth chief, said the manifesto focuses on ensuring the younger generation receives better educational opportunities, quality employment and assistance in owning their first home.

items.title: Can-meh? Yes, new MEH licence plates for Melaka will open for bidding from July 10
items.summary: MELAKA, June 29 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) Melaka has issued a public notice encouraging Malaysians to participate in its latest round of vehicle registration number bidding via the JPJ eBid online platform for plates starting with ‘MEH’ In a Facebook post today, JPJ Melaka urged motorists and vehicle owners to register and submit bids through jpjebid.jpj.gov.my, the department’s official digital bidding system. Bids for MEH plates will start July 10 and close on July 14, with bid results announced on July 15. JPJ eBid, introduced nationwide in recent years, allows users to bid for preferred number plates without visiting JPJ counters.

items.title: High Court rejects former directors’ bid to delay RM492,480 refund owed to umrah pilgrims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The High Court here today dismissed an application by three former directors and shareholders of a tourism company seeking to postpone repayment of RM492,480 to umrah pilgrims pending the outcome of their appeal. Judge Leong Wai Hong dismissed the application by Datuk Dr Fathul Bari Mat Jahya, Sekh Mohd Fazzli Sekh Mohd Ruzi and Wan Azizul Wan Yusoff to stay execution of the payment order. Lawyer Mohd Shahril Madisa represented the three applicants at the proceedings, while lawyer Teoh Bi Shan appeared for KRS Travel Sdn Bhd (KRS), the company that manages the pilgrims’ travel to Makkah.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan says government finalising Engineers Scale of Fees review to set fairer pay benchmark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The government is finalising a review of the Engineers Scale of Fees, which was gazetted in 1998, to ensure that professional engineering service fees are more in line with current economic realities and the operating costs of engineering consultancy firms. He said the amendments would replace the existing maximum fee structure with a new minimum fee benchmark to ensure that engineers receive professional remuneration comparable to that of other professionals in the construction industry. Ahmad said that once gazetted, the revised fee scale will be mandatory for all professional engineers and engineering consultancy practices (ECPs) operating in both the public and private sectors.

items.title: Fire tears through Subang Jaya apartment, four residents rescued after being trapped by thick smoke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Four residents were rescued after a fire gutted a first-floor apartment at Subang Perdana Goodyear Court 7 in USJ today, trapping occupants in an upper-floor unit due to thick smoke. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call at 10.02am and arrived at the apartment block in Jalan USJ 14/1 about 10 minutes later, reported Harian Metro . The fire destroyed an estimated 80 per cent of the first-floor apartment.

items.title: New MOH rule compels medicine suppliers to report shortages from July 1, ensuring stability despite West Asia risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Effective July 1, the Health Ministry (MOH) will enforce mandatory reporting by Product Registration Holders (PRHs) on any disruptions or discontinuation of medicine supply. The ministry said the mechanism is part of the proactive measures being taken to ensure the stability and control of the country’s imported medicine supply chain, despite risks arising from conflicts in West Asia. On medicine security in Sabah’s public healthcare services, the ministry said supply levels in the state remain stable and under control, despite unique logistical challenges due to geographical factors.

items.title: Dungun man accused of hammer attack on grandmother ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 29 — The Sessions Court today ordered a 25-year-old man charged with causing grievous hurt to his grandmother with a hammer to undergo a mental health assessment. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli issued the order against Muhammad Nor Iman Abdullah following an application by the prosecution, which requested that the accused undergo a psychiatric evaluation before any plea or statement is recorded. The court ordered him to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak, to undergo a mental assessment.

items.title: Azalina tables sweeping amendment to Act 792 expanding child sex crime law and closing loopholes overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The proposed amendment to the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 (Act 792) seeks to ensure that Malaysia does not face jurisdictional constraints in taking action against perpetrators of child sexual crimes, even if the offences are committed abroad. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the move is in line with efforts to strengthen protection for children against cross-border sexual crimes, while ensuring the act remains relevant amid the evolving nature of sexual offences. “At the same time, protection is also extended to children who are Malaysian citizens, permanent residents, or ordinarily reside in Malaysia,” she said.

items.title: Two Malaysians injured after IED blast near SUV in Thailand’s Tak Bai, one seriously hurt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Two Malaysians were injured after an improvised explosive device (IED) planted beneath a roadside culvert exploded near their vehicle in Thailand’s Tak Bai district this afternoon, with one of the victims suffering serious injuries. According to Berita Harian, Abdullah suffered shrapnel wounds to his forehead, serious injuries to his left leg and head injuries believed to have been caused by shattered glass, while Muhammad Yusri sustained minor injuries. “The victims’ vehicle was also damaged in the bomb blast.

items.title: Three young Malaysian scientists head to Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting, joining 600 peers worldwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Three young scientists have been selected to represent the country at the 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting, which is being held in Lindau, Germany, from June 28 to July 3. The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (Mosti), in a statement today, announced that the selected representatives are Department of Pharmacology lecturer at the Faculty of Medicine, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Dr Nur Liyana Mohammed Yusof, 34; postdoctoral research fellow at the Department of Physics, Universiti Malaya (UM), Dr Pershaanaa K. Manogran, 29; and postdoctoral fellow at UM Institute of Ocean and Earth Sciences, Dr Ruth Naomi Manuel, 31. According to Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang, Malaysia’s participation in global scientific platforms such as the Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting reflects the government’s commitment to nurturing future-ready talent in line with the aspirations of the 13th Malaysia Plan (2026-2030).

items.title: Puad claims secret Umno‑PAS talks bred overconfidence, now BN faces precarious situation
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Former Johor assembly speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi claimed that the secret plot between Umno and PAS to form a political partnership for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections had led to the former being conceited. Puad, who was a former Umno supreme council member, revealed that the initial agreement required PAS to sit out the Johor state election, paving the way for a Barisan Nasional (BN) victory in all 56 constituencies. “Among the Umno leaders, Datuk Megat (political secretary to the deputy prime minister Datuk Wira Megat Zulkarnain Omardin) believed that if the negotiations were successful Umno can win big in Johor.

items.title: Dzulkefly tells Dewan Rakyat MOH reviewing cerebral palsy registry plan to widen rehab access
items.summary: Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the proposal is aimed at expanding patients’ access to rehabilitation facilities, including through collaboration with the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) rehabilitation centres. “MOH welcomes the proposal to establish a National Cerebral Palsy Registry and is also reviewing efforts to expand patients’ access to rehabilitation facilities, including through collaboration with Perkeso rehabilitation centres,” he said when replying to a question from Dr Kelvin Yii Lee Wuen (PH-Bandar Kuching). Dzulkefly said MOH assists CP patients in obtaining rehabilitation equipment through the Medical Assistance Fund, the Malaysian Medical Welfare Fund and the National Cancer Council, based on clinical assessment and patients’ eligibility.

items.title: Cabinet greenlights MH370 deep-sea search extension with Ocean Infinity for another year, search to resume July 1, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The government has agreed to extend its agreement with UK-based Ocean Infinity for another 12 months to continue deep-sea efforts to locate Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 in the southern Indian Ocean. Transport Minister Loke Siew Fook said the Cabinet approved the extension on June 26, with the new contract period running from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027. Loke said the decision also takes into account Ocean Infinity’s new commercial commitments, which require its main operational assets to be temporarily redeployed elsewhere.

items.title: Ex‑Temerloh police chief ordered to enter defence on 46 bribery charges totalling RM69,600
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 29 — The Sessions Court here today ordered former Temerloh district police chief Mohd Azhar Mohd Yusoff to enter his defence on 46 counts of accepting bribes totalling RM69,600 between 2019 and 2023. “The court finds and rules that the prosecution has successfully established a prima facie case on all 46 principal charges faced by the accused under Section 165 of the Penal Code. Accordingly, the court ordered Mohd Azhar to enter his defence on all the charges.

items.title: PM Anwar to roll out redesigned Malaysian passport tomorrow, holograms and UV ink among 94 features
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to launch the new version of the Malaysian International Passport, equipped with 94 security features, at the Parliament lobby Tuesday. The new passport features a significant enhancement over the current version, which has only 49 security features, making it more secure and difficult to forge. Among the upgraded features are holographic elements, ultraviolet (UV) printing, hidden visual elements and several specialised forensic security features.

items.title: Anwar unveils Malaysia Digital 2030: Targets 30pc GDP, 500,000 jobs, 95pc services online by 2030
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today launched Malaysia Digital 2030 (MD2030), a national action plan for 2026-2030 that marks a major shift in the country’s digital strategy from being a technology consumer to a producer of home-grown innovation. The launch was attended by Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. In a statement, the Digital Ministry said the action plan sets ambitious national targets by 2030, including raising the digital economy’s contribution to gross domestic product (GDP) to 30 per cent, creating 500,000 high-value digital jobs, generating RM4.5 billion in public sector savings through digitalisation, and delivering 95 per cent of government services fully online from end to end.

items.title: Maszlee Malik dismisses MB speculation, says Johor Sultan holds sole prerogative under state constitution
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Pakatan Harapan will leave the appointment of the Johor Menteri Besar entirely to the wisdom and prerogative of the Sultan of Johor should the coalition succeed in forming the state government in the state election. PH’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state seat, Dr Maszlee Malik, said the coalition has not yet discussed the post as its main focus is to provide the best possible service to the people through teamwork. He said this when commenting on speculation circulating on social media linking him as one of the leading candidates for the post of Johor Menteri Besar from the PH bloc should the coalition win the 16th Johor state polls.

items.title: World Cup fever fuels illegal betting surge as police net 331 in nationwide Ops Soga XI crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Police have intensified nationwide enforcement against illegal sports betting ahead of the Fifa World Cup 2026, with more than 300 suspects arrested under an ongoing coordinated operation targeting online gambling activities. Police also seized RM145,005.09 in cash and identified betting transactions worth RM2.63 million linked to the illegal activities. Authorities said 185 websites, applications, platforms and social media accounts were detected promoting or facilitating illegal football betting during the operation.

items.title: Putrajaya says shielding SMEs from war fallout top priority, RM700m in financial help already given up to June
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Bank Negara Malaysia had approved over RM700 million in relief financing up to June, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah told Parliament this morning, as he pledged to make protection of small and medium businesses from the global supply crisis a top priority. The Johor Bahru MP said the fund from BNM’s SME Stabilisation Relief Facility had been extended to some 1,000 SMEs up to June 18. “Affected SMEs received cash flow support through the BNM SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF), totalling RM5 billion...more than RM4 billion remains available, indicating ample room for subsequent applications,” Akmal said during a special communication session in Parliament’s Lower House this morning.

items.title: Kajang cops hunt man suspected of impersonating civil servant to dupe public
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Police are tracking down a man suspected of impersonating a civil servant by wearing a security force uniform and using a background resembling a government office to deceive members of the public. Kajang police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said a report regarding the incident was received at 6.58pm yesterday. Naazron also urged the public to report any suspected criminal activity through the MERS 999 emergency line or the Kajang District Police Control Centre at 03-8911 4243.

items.title: Housing mismatch persists as 32,800 unsold homes worth RM16.3b recorded in Q1 2026, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia is facing a continued mismatch in the housing market, with tens of thousands of completed homes remaining unsold even as affordability challenges persist, the Ministry of Housing and Local Government told Dewan Rakyat today. “This shows that unsold housing is not limited to affordable housing alone, but is also influenced by a broader mismatch between supply and demand across different market segments,” she said. “Generally, D1 to D4 represent lower income households, D5 to D8 middle income, and D9 to D10 high income households,” she said.

items.title: Malaysia’s risk of mass job losses from global energy crisis remains low, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia’s exposure to the risk of mass scale job losses stemming from the global energy supply crunch remains low, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah told Parliament today, even as the crisis is already putting some pressure on local companies. Domestically, Akmal said the supply chokehold is putting pressure on various sectors, particularly logistics, aviation, shipping, agriculture, manufacturing, construction, and trade. When costs rise sharply, their cash flow is more rapidly affected, Akmal said.

items.title: Firefighter pleads not guilty to negligent driving over crash that injured child in Bachok
items.summary: BACHOK, June 29 — A firefighter pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of negligent driving that allegedly resulted in serious injuries to a four-year-old girl early this year. Mohammad Shuhaili Shuib, 46, entered the plea before Magistrate Fakhrul Razzi Abd Hamid. He was charged with negligently driving a Toyota Innova multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) and hitting the girl at the parking area of Sri Chalet Nipah, causing serious injuries to the child, at about 10.45am on January 3 this year.

items.title: LRT3 Shah Alam debuts smoothly, no women-only coach planned yet, says Prasarana
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 29 — The first day of operations for the LRT3 Shah Alam Line got off to a smooth start today, with Prasarana Malaysia Bhd saying only minor teething issues emerged as it began monitoring passenger behaviour to fine-tune the new service. Outgoing Prasarana president and group chief executive officer Amir Hamdan said train operations, station facilities and passenger movement remained under control throughout the morning, with trains operating at peak-hour headways of around eight minutes. Amir said the first day of operations also gave Prasarana an opportunity to observe passenger flow, particularly at interchange stations where commuters transfer between the Shah Alam Line and existing rail services.

items.title: Malaysia eyes AI, drones to strengthen maritime security, says deputy home minister
items.summary: He said the overall wellbeing of personnel and the condition of operational assets remain strong, supported by ongoing upgrades in capability and coordinated enforcement operations to maintain maritime security. “This is achieved through enhanced operational capabilities supported by sufficient assets and the implementation of enforcement operations to ensure maritime security is consistently maintained. Shamsul Anuar said all enforcement units remain on standby and ready for immediate deployment when required.
